evidence: R1 Serial0/1/0 configuration shows ip access-group in R1_IN and ip access-group out R1_TO_R2 (R1 running-config).
Access-list R1_IN entry sequence 10: action=deny, protocol=icmp, ipv4-address=172.16.0.0 mask=0.0.0.255, dst-eq/port='echo' (R1 access_lists in running-config).
Alert: source 192.168.0.11 -> destination 172.16.0.11: 25 pings sent, 0 successful (alert_description).
R1 running-config: access_lists -> name: "R1_IN" -> sequence 10 ace-rule: action: "deny", protocol: "icmp", ipv4-address: "172.16.0.0", mask: "0.0.0.255", dst-eq-port2: "echo"
R1 running-config: access_lists -> name: "R1_IN" -> sequence 20 ace-rule: action: "permit", protocol: "ip", any -> (permit ip any any after the deny)
R1 running-config (current): interfaces -> Serial 0/1/0 -> ip.access-group -> out -> acl-name: "R1_TO_R2" (no ip access-group in shown)
previous_changes: tool_call detach_acl_from_interface for router1 interface Serial0/1/0 direction 'in' returned success ("ACL fjernet fra in på Serial0/1/0")

missing_info: Was R1_IN the ACL that was attached inbound on R1 Serial0/1/0 at the time the pings failed (provide the pre-change interface access-group or a timestamped running-config snapshot)?

What exact ACL name was removed by the recorded detach_acl_from_interface previous_changes entry (tool_call_id: call_QEN1aMmG3sYiEbHdhsszXaAy) and when was that change made relative to the observed ping failures?

plan_history.intent_description: R1 inbound ACL R1_IN on Serial0/1/0 denies ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24, causing pings from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11 to fail; after remediation, ICMP between these hosts should be allowed.
plan_history.problem: Access-list R1_IN on R1 (applied inbound on Serial0/1/0) contains a deny icmp entry for 172.16.0.0/24 which drops ICMP responses from 172.16.0.11, resulting in 0/25 ping success.
plan_history.fix_summary: Remove the deny icmp entry (sequence 10) from access-list R1_IN on router1 so ICMP between 172.16.0.11 and 192.168.0.11 is permitted.

plan_history.intent_description: R1 inbound ACL R1_IN contains an extended deny ICMP for source 172.16.0.0/24 applied on Serial0/1/0, blocking ICMP replies from 172.16.0.11 to 192.168.0.11; after remediation, ICMP between these hosts must be allowed.
plan_history.problem: ACL R1_IN on router1 contains an extended ACE (sequence 10) that denies ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24; when applied inbound on Serial0/1/0 this blocks ping replies and causes reachability failure.
plan_history.fix_summary: Remove the explicit deny ICMP ACE (sequence 10) from ACL R1_IN on router1 so ICMP traffic from 172.16.0.0/24 is permitted (the subsequent permit ip any any will allow the traffic).
